NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble; May the name of the God of Jacob defend you;
NKJV.2: May He send you help from the sanctuary, And strengthen you out of Zion;
NKJV.3: May He remember all your offerings, And accept your burnt sacrifice. Selah
NKJV.4: May He grant you according to your heart’s desire, And fulfill all your purpose.
NKJV.5: We will rejoice in your salvation, And in the name of our God we will set up our banners! May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
NKJV.6: Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed; He will answer him from His holy heaven With the saving strength of His right hand.
NKJV.7: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses; But we will remember the name of the LORD our God.
NKJV.8: They have bowed down and fallen; But we have risen and stand upright.
NKJV.9: Save, LORD! May the King answer us when we call.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. The LORD<S>3068</S> hear<S>6030</S> thee in the day<S>3117</S> of trouble<S>6869</S>; the name<S>8034</S> of the God<S>430</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S> defend<S>7682</S> thee; <sup>defend: Heb. set thee on an high place</sup>
KJV.2: Send<S>7971</S> thee help<S>5828</S> from the sanctuary<S>6944</S>, and strengthen<S>5582</S> thee out of Zion<S>6726</S>; <sup>thee help: Heb. thy help</sup> <sup>strengthen: Heb. support</sup>
KJV.3: Remember<S>2142</S> all thy offerings<S>4503</S>, and accept<S>1878</S> thy burnt sacrifice<S>5930</S>; Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>accept: Heb. turn to ashes: or, make fat</sup>
KJV.4: Grant<S>5414</S> thee according to thine own heart<S>3824</S>, and fulfil<S>4390</S> all thy counsel<S>6098</S>.
KJV.5: We will rejoice<S>7442</S> in thy salvation<S>3444</S>, and in the name<S>8034</S> of our God<S>430</S> we will set up our banners<S>1713</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> fulfil<S>4390</S> all thy petitions<S>4862</S>.
KJV.6: Now know<S>3045</S> I that the LORD<S>3068</S> saveth<S>3467</S> his anointed<S>4899</S>; he will hear<S>6030</S> him from his holy<S>6944</S> heaven<S>8064</S> with the saving<S>3468</S> strength<S>1369</S> of his right hand<S>3225</S>. <sup>his holy: Heb. the heaven of his holiness</sup> <sup>with: Heb. by the strength of the salvation of</sup>
KJV.7: Some trust in chariots<S>7393</S>, and some in horses<S>5483</S>: but we will remember<S>2142</S> the name<S>8034</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> our God<S>430</S>.
KJV.8: They are brought down<S>3766</S> and fallen<S>5307</S>: but we are risen<S>6965</S>, and stand upright<S>5749</S>.
KJV.9: Save<S>3467</S>, LORD<S>3068</S>: let the king<S>4428</S> hear<S>6030</S> us when<S>3117</S> we call<S>7121</S>.

ESV.1: May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob protect you!
ESV.2: May he send you help from the sanctuary and give you support from Zion!
ESV.3: May he remember all your offerings and regard with favor your burnt sacrifices! Selah
ESV.4: May he grant you your heart's desire and fulfill all your plans!
ESV.5: May we shout for joy over your salvation, and in the name of our God set up our banners! May the LORD fulfill all your petitions!
ESV.6: Now I know that the LORD saves his anointed; he will answer him from his holy heaven with the saving might of his right hand.
ESV.7: Some trust in chariots and some in horses, but we trust in the name of the LORD our God.
ESV.8: They collapse and fall, but we rise and stand upright.
ESV.9: O LORD, save the king! May he answer us when we call.

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. May the LORD answer you in the day of trouble! May the name of the God of Jacob set you [securely] on high!
NASB.2: May He send you help from the sanctuary And support you from Zion!
NASB.3: May He remember all your meal offerings And find your burnt offering acceptable! Selah.
NASB.4: May He grant you your heart's desire And fulfill all your counsel!
NASB.5: We will sing for joy over your victory, And in the name of our God we will set up our banners. May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
NASB.6: Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed; He will answer him from His holy heaven With the saving strength of His right hand.
NASB.7: Some [boast] in chariots and some in horses, But we will boast in the name of the LORD, our God.
NASB.8: They have bowed down and fallen, But we have risen and stood upright.
NASB.9: Save, O LORD; May the King answer us in the day we call.

NLT.1:
In times of trouble, may the LORD answer your cry.
May the name of the God of Jacob keep you safe from all harm.
NLT.2:
May he send you help from his sanctuary
and strengthen you from Jerusalem.
NLT.3:
May he remember all your gifts
and look favorably on your burnt offerings. Interlude
NLT.4:
May he grant your heart’s desires
and make all your plans succeed.
NLT.5:
May we shout for joy when we hear of your victory
and raise a victory banner in the name of our God.
May the LORD answer all your prayers.
NLT.6:
Now I know that the LORD rescues his anointed king.
He will answer him from his holy heaven
and rescue him by his great power.
NLT.7:
Some nations boast of their chariots and horses,
but we boast in the name of the LORD our God.
NLT.8:
Those nations will fall down and collapse,
but we will rise up and stand firm.
NLT.9:
Give victory to our king, O LORD!
Answer our cry for help.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. May Yahweh answer you in the day of trouble. May the name of the God of Jacob set you up on high,
WEB.2: send you help from the sanctuary, grant you support from Zion,
WEB.3: remember all your offerings, and accept your burned sacrifice. Selah.
WEB.4: May He grant you your heart’s desire, and fulfill all your counsel.
WEB.5: We will triumph in your salvation. In the name of our God, we will set up our banners. May Yahweh grant all your requests.
WEB.6: Now I know that Yahweh saves his anointed. He will answer him from his holy heaven, with the saving strength of his right hand.
WEB.7: Some trust in chariots, and some in horses, but we trust the name of Yahweh our God.
WEB.8: They are bowed down and fallen, but we rise up, and stand upright.
WEB.9: Save, Yahweh! Let the King answer us when we call!

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A psalm of David. [1] May the LORD answer you in a day of trouble; ⓕ
may the name of Jacob’s God protect you. ⓖ
CSB17.2:
May he send you help from the sanctuary
and sustain you from Zion. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
May he remember all your offerings
and accept your burnt offering. ⓘ Selah
CSB17.4:
May he give you what your heart desires ⓙ
and fulfill your whole purpose. ⓚ
CSB17.5:
Let us shout for joy at your victory ⓛ
and lift the banner in the name of our God. ⓜ
May the LORD fulfill all your requests. ⓝ
CSB17.6:
Now I know that the LORD gives victory to his anointed; ⓐ
he will answer him from his holy heaven
with mighty victories from his right hand. ⓑ
CSB17.7:
Some take pride in chariots, and others in horses,
but we take pride in the name of the LORD our God. ⓒ
CSB17.8:
They collapse and fall,
but we rise and stand firm. ⓓ
CSB17.9:
LORD, give victory to the king! ⓔ
May he [1] answer us on the day that we call.

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. May the LORD answer you (David) in the day of trouble!
May the name of the God of Jacob set you securely on high [and defend you in battle]!
AMP.2:
May He send you help from the sanctuary (His dwelling place)
And support and strengthen you from Zion!
AMP.3:
May He remember all your meal offerings
And accept your burnt offering. Selah.
AMP.4:
May He grant you your heart's desire
And fulfill all your plans.
AMP.5:
We will sing joyously over your victory,
And in the name of our God we will set up our banners.
May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
AMP.6:
Now I know that the LORD saves His anointed;
He will answer him from His holy heaven
With the saving strength of His right hand.
AMP.7:
Some trust in chariots and some in horses,
But we will remember and trust in the name of the LORD our God.
AMP.8:
They have bowed down and fallen,
But we have risen and stood upright.
AMP.9:
O LORD, save [the king];
May the King answer us in the day we call.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God answer you on the day you crash,
The name God-of-Jacob put you out of harm's reach,
MSG.2:
Send reinforcements from Holy Hill,
Dispatch from Zion fresh supplies,
MSG.3:
Exclaim over your offerings,
Celebrate your sacrifices,
MSG.4:
Give you what your heart desires,
Accomplish your plans.
MSG.5:
When you win, we plan to raise the roof
and lead the parade with our banners.
May all your wishes come true!
MSG.6:
That clinches it—help's coming,
an answer's on the way,
everything's going to work out.
MSG.7:
See those people polishing their chariots,
and those others grooming their horses?
But we're making garlands for God our God.
MSG.8:
The chariots will rust,
those horses pull up lame—
and we'll be on our feet, standing tall.
MSG.9:
Make the king a winner, God;
the day we call, give us your answer.

NIV.1:
Psalm 20
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
May the Lord answer you when you are in distress;
may the name of the God of Jacob protect you.
NIV.2:
May he send you help from the sanctuary
and grant you support from Zion.
NIV.3:
May he remember all your sacrifices
and accept your burnt offerings. Selah
NIV.4:
May he give you the desire of your heart
and make all your plans succeed.
NIV.5:
We will shout for joy when you are victorious
and will lift up our banners in the name of our God.
May the Lord grant all your requests.
NIV.6:
Now I know that the Lord saves his anointed;
he answers him from his holy heaven
with the saving power of his right hand.
NIV.7:
Some trust in chariots and some in horses,
but we trust in the name of the Lord our God.
NIV.8:
They are brought to their knees and fall,
but we rise up and stand firm.
NIV.9:
O Lord, save the king!
Answer us when we call!

NRSVCE.1:
The LORD answer you in the day of trouble!
The name of the God of Jacob protect you!
NRSVCE.2:
May he send you help from the sanctuary,
and give you support from Zion.
NRSVCE.3:
May he remember all your offerings,
and regard with favor your burnt sacrifices. Selah
NRSVCE.4:
May he grant you your heart’s desire,
and fulfill all your plans.
NRSVCE.5:
May we shout for joy over your victory,
and in the name of our God set up our banners.
May the LORD fulfill all your petitions.
NRSVCE.6:
Now I know that the LORD will help his anointed;
he will answer him from his holy heaven
with mighty victories by his right hand.
NRSVCE.7:
Some take pride in chariots, and some in horses,
but our pride is in the name of the LORD our God.
NRSVCE.8:
They will collapse and fall,
but we shall rise and stand upright.
NRSVCE.9:
Give victory to the king, O LORD;
answer us when we call.

NET.1: May the LORD answer you when you are in trouble; may the God of Jacob make you secure!
NET.2: May he send you help from his temple; from Zion may he give you support!
NET.3: May he take notice of your offerings; may he accept your burnt sacrifice! (Selah)
NET.4: May he grant your heart’s desire; may he bring all your plans to pass!
NET.5: Then we will shout for joy over your victory; we will rejoice in the name of our God! May the LORD grant all your requests!
NET.6: Now I am sure that the LORD will deliver his chosen king; he will intervene for him from his holy heavenly temple, and display his mighty ability to deliver.
NET.7: Some trust in chariots and others in horses, but we depend on the LORD our God.
NET.8: They will fall down, but we will stand firm.
NET.9: The LORD will deliver the king; he will answer us when we call to him for help!
